zh.lifeSummary: 當Cecilia Savage於大約1465年出生在愛爾蘭武爾斯特時，她的父親Robert Savage18歲，母親Lady Helen Dethick18歲。她於大約1480年與John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens在愛爾蘭庫迪 安特里姆安特里姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1495年在英國蘇格蘭Comhairle nan Eilean Siar享年31歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cecilia Savage于大约1465年出生在爱尔兰武爾斯特时，她的父亲Robert Savage18岁，母亲Lady Helen Dethick18岁。她于大约1480年与John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens在爱尔兰庫迪 安特里姆安特里姆结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1495年在英国苏格兰Comhairle nan Eilean Siar享年31岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: Cecilia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

variants.name: Cecelia
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

en.lifeSummary: When Cecilia Savage was born about 1465, in Ulster, Ireland, her father, Robert Savage, was 18 and her mother, Lady Helen Dethick, was 18. She married John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens about 1480, in Antrim, County Antrim, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1495, in Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 31, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cecilia Savage est née environ 1465, à Ulster, Irlande, son père, Robert Savage, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Lady Helen Dethick, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens environ 1480, à Antrim, County Antrim, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1495, à Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Cecilia Savage um 1465 in Ulster, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Savage 18 und ihre Mutter Lady Helen Dethick 18. Sie hat um 1480 in Antrim, County Antrim, Irland John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1495 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Cecilia Savage è nata il circa 1465 nel Ulster, Irlanda, sua padre, Robert Savage, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Lady Helen Dethick, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens circa 1480, nel Antrim, County Antrim, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1495, nel Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ko.Dunnyveg, Islay, Western Isles, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 Comhairle nan Eilean Siar
ko.lifeSummary: Cecilia Savage(이)가 대략 1465년 아일랜드 울스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Savage(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Helen Dethick(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1480년 아일랜드 카운티 앤트림 앤트림에서 John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 영국 스코틀랜드 Comhairle nan Eilean Siar에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Cecilia Savage nasceu aproximadamente 1465, em Ulster, Irlanda, seu pai, Robert Savage, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Lady Helen Dethick, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens aproximadamente 1480, em Antrim, County Antrim, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1495, em Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 31 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.Dunnyveg, Islay, Western Isles, Scotland: Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cecilia Savage родилась около 1465 г. в местности улстир, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Robert Savage было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Helen Dethick, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens около 1480 г. в местности антрим, коунти антрим, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1495 г. в местности Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 31 год и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

Murió en 1495, en Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
